pr.title: grdisplay cleanup and near match of grDisplay_801C5DB0
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Cleaned up grdisplay and added a near-match implementation of grDisplay_801C5DB0. The PR retyped and clarified grDisplay_801C5B90, added proper grdisplay/inlines/fog/state includes, modeled stage display gating around camera, fog, fighter shadows, and JObj callbacks, typed Ground_801C1E84 as returning HSD_GObj*, and replaced a local CObj view-matrix inline in jobj.c with HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtrDirect. decomp.dev reported grDisplay_801C5DB0 improved to 99.58% and grDisplay_801C5B90 to 99.85%; there was no human review text in the slice.

key_files.role: Primary change: cleaned grDisplay_801C5B90 and added near-match grDisplay_801C5DB0 stage display logic using camera checks, fog state, shadow updates, JObj traversal, and display callbacks.
Changed Ground_801C1E84 return type from void* to HSD_GObj*, matching its stage_info.x12C usage as a fog gobj.
Header declaration updated to expose Ground_801C1E84 as HSD_GObj* instead of void*.
Removed local HSD_CObjGetViewMtx inline and used HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtrDirect in HSD_JObjDispAll.

what_changed: Added `#include "grdisplay.h"`, `#include "inlines.h"`, `#include "lb/forward.h"`, `<baselib/fog.h>`, and `<baselib/state.h>` to grdisplay.c so the file uses project declarations and typed GET_* helpers instead of ad hoc local knowledge.
Changed grDisplay_801C5B90's signature from generic `void* arg1, u32 arg2, int arg3` style to `Mtx vmtx, u32 flags, u32 rendermode`, matching HSD_JObj display conventions.

Reworked grDisplay_801C5B90 to use named JObj flag constants `JOBJ_INSTANCE` and `JOBJ_HIDDEN`, recurse over child/next JObjs, compute instance matrices with HSD_MtxInverseConcat and PSMTXConcat, and update fighter LbShadow state before HSD_JObjDisp.

Added grDisplay_801C5DB0, which gets the Ground from the gobj, gates display by `gp->x11_flags.b012 == Camera_8003108C()`, checks `gp->x18`, current camera CObj, and `Ground_801C2C8C`, then handles fog reset/state invalidation and dispatches either the custom grDisplay_801C5B90 path or HSD_GObj_JObjCallback.
In grDisplay_801C5DB0, the non-custom display path clears each fighter shadow flag and calls lbShadow_8000EEE0 before the standard JObj callback.
In grDisplay_801C5DB0, fog is cleared with HSD_FogSet(0) when `gp->x10_flags.b2 == 0`, and later restored from `GET_FOG(Ground_801C1E84())` when fog display state is active.
Changed Ground_801C1E84 from returning `void*` to returning `HSD_GObj*`, allowing typed use as a fog gobj in grdisplay.
Removed the local inline `HSD_CObjGetViewMtx` in baselib/jobj.c and replaced its only shown use with `HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtrDirect(cobj)`.
smart_moves: Recovered types first: using `Mtx`, `u32 flags`, `u32 rendermode`, `HSD_GObj*`, `HSD_Fog*`, and GET_* macros made the stage display code read like the engine APIs it calls and reduced anonymous casts.
Reused the existing direct CObj view-matrix accessor instead of keeping a one-off inline, aligning grdisplay and HSD_JObjDispAll around the same API and likely improving codegen consistency.

Preserved original-looking control flow in grDisplay_801C5DB0 with early returns and a `skip` label around the gp->x18/current-camera checks; this is a common useful tactic for near-matching branch layout.
Kept stage rendering side effects in a careful order: camera eligibility checks, optional fog clear, HSD_StateInvalidate, shadow preparation or custom recursive display, then optional fog restore.
Used named JObj flags for readability while preserving the unusual render-selection bit tests `(flags << 18)` and `(flags << 28)`, avoiding over-abstraction of match-sensitive behavior.
decomp_lessons: For display code, prefer matching the baselib signatures already used by HSD_JObjDisp/HSD_JObjDispAll: `Mtx vmtx`, `flags`, and `rendermode` carry more matching and documentation value than `void* argN` placeholders.

Stage display functions can depend on global camera state, the current CObj, fog globals, and fighter shadow side effects; future decomp should preserve these side effects and their ordering before trying to simplify control flow.
If a ground helper returns a gobj stored in stage_info, type it as `HSD_GObj*` in both source and header; this enables typed access through `hsd_obj` and GET_* macros.
Do not introduce local inline accessors for simple struct fields when a canonical baselib helper exists; using the shared helper can improve consistency across translation units.
Unused locals or padding arrays in near-match code may be intentional stack/register-shaping artifacts; remove them only with match evidence.

The bot-reported match gains included many grZebes items even though the PR touched grdisplay/ground/jobj; future analysis should verify object-order or dependency effects before attributing those stage matches to a specific source edit.
naming_conventions: Address-based function names such as `grDisplay_801C5DB0`, `grDisplay_801C5B90`, and `Ground_801C1E84` were retained because better semantic names were not evidenced.
Recovered internal names used semantic engine terms: `vmtx`, `flags`, `rendermode`, `camgobj`, `foggobj`, `fighter`, `shadow`, and `vec`.
HSD object pointers keep project type prefixes: `HSD_GObj*`, `HSD_JObj*`, `HSD_CObj*`, `HSD_Fog*`, `HSD_PObj*`, and `HSD_ShapeAnim*`.
GET_* macros are the preferred convention for typed object payload access from gobj wrappers: `GET_GROUND`, `GET_COBJ`, `GET_JOBJ`, and `GET_FOG`.
Unrecovered Ground fields remained offset/bitfield names such as `x10_flags.b2`, `x10_flags.b3`, `x10_flags.b5`, `x11_flags.b012`, `x18`, `stage_info.x17C`, and `stage_info.x12C`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted a null early-return in grDisplay_801C5B90 into an outer `if (jobj != NULL)` body, which can better match original branch structure.
Used a `goto skip` in grDisplay_801C5DB0 to share the draw path after alternate gp->x18/current-object checks, an explicit control-flow shaping tactic visible in the diff.
Left unused locals such as `u32 unused[6]` in grDisplay_801C5B90 and `HSD_Fog* fog; int i;` in grDisplay_801C5DB0, suggesting stack/register layout was being preserved for the near match.
Replaced direct `cobj->view_mtx` access through a deleted local inline with `HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtrDirect(cobj)` in PSMTXConcat calls.

Matched JObj instance traversal by setting up parent and child matrices, inverse-concatenating child against parent, concatenating with the current CObj viewing matrix, and recursing into the child with the computed matrix.
review_feedback: No human review comments or GitHub review records were present in the PR slice.
decomp-dev bot reported matched code 55.77% (+0.15%, +5868 bytes) and matched data 34.78% (+0.07%, +832 bytes).

decomp-dev specifically reported `main/melee/gr/grdisplay` `.data` reached 100.00% (+12 bytes), `grDisplay_801C5DB0` improved by +430 bytes to 99.58%, and `grDisplay_801C5B90` improved by +5 bytes to 99.85%.
The same bot report listed many grZebes matches and improvements; the slice does not provide human explanation for why this grdisplay cleanup affected those reported items.
follow_up_queries: Find the later PR, if any, that finishes `grDisplay_801C5DB0` from 99.58% to 100% and compare the remaining control-flow or stack-shaping changes.
Search for other local helpers or raw `cobj->view_mtx` accesses that should use `HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtrDirect`.
Audit all `Ground_801C1E84` callers after the return type change to confirm they consistently treat `stage_info.x12C` as an `HSD_GObj*` fog object.
Investigate why the decomp.dev report showed broad `grZebes` match gains from a PR whose changed files were grdisplay, ground, and jobj.
Look for adjacent grdisplay functions using generic `void* argN` signatures that can be retyped to HSD display conventions.
